# @stabgan/openrouter-mcp-multimodal

> MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that gives any MCP client — Claude Desktop, Cursor, Kiro, VS Code, Windsurf, Cline — access to 300+ LLMs through OpenRouter. Covers text chat plus the full multimodal matrix: image / audio / video analysis and image / audio / video generation (Veo 3.1, Sora 2 Pro, Seedance, Wan).

## Install

- npm: `npx -y @stabgan/openrouter-mcp-multimodal`
- Docker: `docker run --rm -i -e OPENROUTER_API_KEY=... stabgan/openrouter-mcp-multimodal:latest`
- Smithery: `npx -y @smithery/cli install @stabgan/openrouter-mcp-multimodal --client claude`

Get a free API key at https://openrouter.ai/keys.

## Tools

- `chat_completion` — send messages to any OpenRouter model
- `analyze_image` — vision analysis of local files, URLs, or data URIs (sharp-optimized)
- `analyze_audio` — transcribe / analyze audio (WAV, MP3, FLAC, OGG, …)
- `analyze_video` — analyze video (mp4, mpeg, mov, webm)
- `generate_image` — text-to-image with `aspect_ratio` (14 values), `image_size` (0.5K–4K), `max_tokens`
- `generate_audio` — text-to-speech / conversational audio / music generation
- `generate_video` — async text-to-video (Veo 3.1, Sora 2 Pro, Seedance, Wan)
- `get_video_status` — resume a video-generation job by id
- `search_models` — filter OpenRouter models by name, provider, or modality
- `get_model_info` — pricing / context length / capabilities for any model
- `validate_model` — check if a model id exists

All error responses carry `_meta.code` from a closed taxonomy: `INVALID_INPUT`, `UNSAFE_PATH`, `UPSTREAM_HTTP`, `UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT`, `UPSTREAM_REFUSED`, `UNSUPPORTED_FORMAT`, `RESOURCE_TOO_LARGE`, `ZDR_INCOMPATIBLE`, `MODEL_NOT_FOUND`, `JOB_FAILED`, `JOB_STILL_RUNNING`, `INTERNAL`.

## Security

- IPv4 + IPv6 SSRF blocklist (loopback, private, mapped, compat, multicast, 6to4, Teredo, ORCHID)
- Output path sandbox for `save_path`, overridable with `OPENROUTER_ALLOW_UNSAFE_PATHS=1`
- Retry-After-aware HTTP retries with jitter
- MCP 2025 tool annotations (`readOnlyHint` / `destructiveHint` / `idempotentHint`)
